reasoning:
The government of Zimbabwe recently made a $3.1 million cash layout in early April 2025, labeled specifically as a 'first batch' covering 378 out of 740 vetted and approved farms. Administratively, this leaves a pre-approved queue of 362 farmers awaiting payment. Having an already-approved pipeline makes a subsequent payment within the upcoming 6-7 week window (before June 1, 2025) highly probable, as it is likely just a matter of continued banking processing rather than requiring a whole new budget allocation or funding tranche. Furthermore, the payouts are highly publicized as part of a debt resolution framework to mend ties with the West, giving the administration strong political motivation to complete the promised batch.

However, the probability is constrained from being excessively high due to two factors: (1) Sovereign bureaucratic processing (especially in economically distressed states) can face sudden delays or liquidity issues, and (2) The Compensation Steering Committee (CSC) publicly rejected the agreement in mid-April, which could lead some of the remaining farmers to halt their paperwork or refuse the interim deposits. Even so, since the question only requires 'an additional payment'—which would be triggered by paying even a single remaining farmer from the queue—the likelihood remains tipped favorably toward a YES.
